rulings.Case Title: STEVEN ECHTMAN VS. VIRTUAL LEGAL PROCEEDINGS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 18, 2024, Line 10, PLAINTIFF STEVEN ECHTMAN's Motion for Leave to File Amended Complaint. Off calendar. Untimely failed per CCP 1005. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: ONEMAIN FINANCIAL GROUP, LLC, AS SERVICER FOR VS. MARCELO SAMAME ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of And Motion For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Stipulation Of The Parties
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 18, 2024, Line 2, PLAINTIFF ONEMAIN FINANCIAL GROUP, LLC, AS SERVICER FOR ('ASF') WILMINGTON TRUST, N.A., AS ISSUER LOAN TRUSTEE FOR ONEMAIN FINANCIAL ISSUANCE TRUST 2018-1's Motion For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Stipulation Of The Parties. Plaintiff ONEMAIN FINANCIAL GROUP, LLC's Motion For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Stipulation Of The Parties to which no opposition was filed is GRANTED. Good cause appearing, the court will enter Judgment in favor of Plaintiff ONEMAIN FINANCIAL GROUP, LLC and against Defendant MARCELO SAMAME ("Defendant") pursuant to the stipulation of the Parties. Plaintiff ONEMAIN FINANCIAL GROUP, LLC's must promptly lodge with this department a proposed order consistent with this ruling and, separately, a proposed judgment. The proposed judgment shall not include counsel's or the represented party's name or information in the upper left-hand corner of the caption page. A Judicial Council form may be used if appropriate. Submission consistent with the Code and Rules of Court. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS A. KARAS ET AL VS. ANDREAS ANGELOPOULOS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants Appearance At Deposition; To Deem Request For Admission Admitted; And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery calendar for Tuesday, December 17, 2024 Line 1. PLAINTIFF THOMAS KARAS AS TRUSTEE OF THE THOMAS A. KARAS AND ELENI KARAS REVOCABLE TRUST DATED 7/10/2014, ELENI KARA's Motion To Compel Defendants Appearance At Deposition; To Deem Request For Admission Admitted; And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Plaintiff Thomas A. Karas's Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants Appearance At Deposition; To Deem Request For Admission Admitted; And Request For Monetary Sanctions to which no opposition has been filed is GRANTED. Defendant Andreas Angelopoulos is ordered to appear at deposition in this matter no later than January 10, 2025, such deposition may be conducted remotely and shall not exceed four hours but may be spread across two sessions. Karas's conduct related to this discovery issue has been quite reasonable and Angelopoulos's conduct has been unreasonable. Given Angelopoulos's complete failure to respond to other written discovery, deposition by response to written interrogatories is not a realistic option in this case. The requests for admissions served August 28, 2024, are deemed admitted. Angelopoulos's failure to respond was neither reasonable nor justified. Angelopoulos's response to the subject discovery was not substantially justified. Angelopoulos and his counsel shall pay a total of $3,200 to Plaintiff as sanctions. Plaintiff is advised that stacked discovery motions are disfavored. Stacked motions may be ordered off calendar in favor of individual motions focused each on a single discovery vehicle (e.g., Frog Set, SRog Set, RFP Set, RFA Set, deposition notice) addressed to a single party. For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address.= (301/JMQ).

rulings.Case Title: KIDS EMPIRE CHELTENHAM (PA) LLC, VS. REED SMITH LLP, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff Kids Empire Cheltenham (Pa) Llc'S First Set Of Form Interrogatories To Defendant Joseph M. Marger And For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of At Least $1000 Against Joseph M. Marger; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery calendar for Tuesday, December 17, 2024 Line 3. 2 - PLAINTIFF KIDS EMPIRE CHELTENHAM (PA) LLC's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff Kids Empire Cheltenham (Pa) Llc'S First Set Of Form Interrogatories To Defendant Joseph M. Marger And For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of At Least $1000 Against Joseph M. Marger. Plaintiff's motions to compel further responses to form interrogatory 15.1 is GRANTED. Defendants Reed Smith, LLP, Joseph Marger, and Samantha Beatty shall serve on plaintiff's counsel full and complete supplemental responses to form interrogatory 15.1 by January 6, 2024. Reed Smith, LLP, Joseph Marger, and Samantha Beatty shall collectively pay $1000 to Plaintiff by January 6, 2024, for the three motions to compel because they did not act with substantial justification. "Each answer in the response must be 'as complete and straightforward as the information reasonably available to the responding party permits. If an interrogatory cannot be answered completely, it shall be answered to the extent possible.'" Weil, et al., Cal. Practice Guide: Civ. Pro. Before Trial (The Rutter Group 2024) 8:1047 (italics in original, quoting Code Civ. Proc. 2030.220(a)). Here, "discovery has just begun" is not a proper response to the interrogatory. Defendants are obliged to state the facts that support their denials and defenses that they are presently aware of. If defendants presently do not have any facts, then they should say so. Defendants can reasonably ascertain the material allegations. (See Burke v. Superior Ct. of Sacramento Cnty. (1969) 71 Cal.2d 276, 281 ["a defendant in California courts may be required through discovery to disclose not only the evidentiary facts underlying his affirmative defenses . . . and denials . . . but also whether or not he makes a particular contention, either as to the facts or as to the possible issues in the case."].) The parties are advised if discovery disputes persist, the court, at the parties' expense, may appoint a discovery referee. As to each motion, the prevailing party must submit a proposed order (here, three orders) and must do so before the time for the hearing on the motion. For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ).
